sections.content:

Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

- Action semantics: {'MOUSE': 'Clicks on a grid position. None of the clicks so far have changed the yellow shapes - only node IDs changed and timer bar shrunk.'}
- Object taxonomy: {'checkerboard': 'B and c alternating blocks forming background', 'yellow_top': '14px shape at rows 13-16, cols 30-33. Solid block with c-colored gap at row 16 cols 31-32', 'yellow_bottom': '16px U-shape at rows 33-37, cols 29-34. Open at top, filled bottom row', 'gap_piece_top': "c-colored 2px piece at [[16,31],[16,32]] in top shape's gap", 'blue_character': 'Blue player avatar at bottom', 'blue_bar_top': 'Blue bar at row 1, shrinks with each action', 'indicator_area': 'Gray/white rectangles below board at rows 39-45, possibly interactive buttons'}
- HUD map: {'top_blue_bar': 'Timer/progress bar at row 1'}
Current level:
- World model: Checkerboard with 2 yellow shapes. Top: 14px solid block with c-gap at bottom. Bottom: 16px U-shape. Indicator buttons below board. No clicks on yellow shapes have changed them.
- Goal model: Unknown. May involve the indicator buttons below the board rather than clicking on the shapes directly.
- Recent findings: ['Clicking (35,31) on bottom U did NOT change yellow shapes', 'Clicking (15,31), (16,31), (13,31) on/around top U did NOT change shapes', 'Indicator area at rows 39-45 has gray/white rectangles that may be interactive']
- Open questions: ['What do the indicator buttons do?', 'Is the goal to interact with buttons below the board?', 'What is the relationship between the two yellow shapes?']
- Plan: Try clicking on the indicator buttons below the board to see if they affect the shapes
- Strategy log: ['Clicked on yellow shapes directly (gap and body) - no effect on shapes. Need to try indicator buttons below board.']
- Failed probes: ['MOUSE(35,31): no change to yellow shapes (step 1)', 'MOUSE(15,31): no change to top U (step 2)', 'MOUSE(16,31): no change to top U (step 3)', 'MOUSE(13,31): no change to top U (step 4)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model.

When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking the blue character at (55,36) didn't seem to change anything visible. Let me check what changed. I need to reconsider the puzzle entirely.
Let me think about what I haven't tried:
1. I've clicked on yellow shapes - no effect
2. I've clicked on buttons - toggles color
3. I've clicked on the gap - no effect
4. I've clicked on the blue character - need to check
Maybe the goal involves clicking on the vertical stems (rows 44-46) rather than the horizontal bars? Or maybe I need to click on specific checkerboard cells?
Let me look at the full segmentation to understand what objects are on the board and what I haven't tried clicking.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking blue character
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Check if blue character changed
b_blues = [(n['id'], n['color'], n['pixels'], n['hash']) for n in before.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b']
a_blues = [(n['id'], n['color'], n['pixels'], n['hash']) for n in after.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b']
print(f"Blue objects before: {b_blues}")
print(f"Blue objects after: {a_blues}")

# Check all nodes in current frame - what interactive elements exist?
print("\nAll non-checkerboard, non-timer objects:")
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    bnd = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in bnd)
    if n['color'] not in ('B', 'c', 'b') or n['pixels'] < 20:
        if min_r > 2:  # skip timer bar
            print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, bnd={bnd}")

# Let me try clicking on the vertical stems instead of horizontal bars
# Stem positions: (44-46, 21), (44-46, 26), (44-46, 31), (44-46, 36), (44-46, 41)
# Try clicking stem 1
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 21}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"MOUSE": "Clicking button toggles B↔w. Clicking shapes/gap/blue character has no visible effect."}, "object_taxonomy": {"yellow_top": "14px solid block with c-gap", "yellow_bottom": "16px U-shape", "indicator_buttons": "5 toggle buttons at row 42", "blue_character": "Blue object at bottom bounds [51,32]-[59,40]"}, "hud_map": {"top_blue_bar": "Timer bar at row 1"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Buttons at B,w,B,w,w. Tried clicking shapes, gap, blue character - no effect.", "goal_model": "Unknown - need to try stems or other interactive elements", "recent_findings": ["Yellow shapes never change", "Blue character click had no visible effect"], "open_questions": ["Do stems do something different from buttons?"], "current_plan": "Try clicking vertical stems", "strategy_log": ["Button toggling, shape clicking, gap clicking, blue character clicking - none completed level"], "failed_probes": ["MOUSE(35,31)", "MOUSE(15,31)", "MOUSE(16,31)", "MOUSE(13,31)", "MOUSE(16,31) gap", "MOUSE(55,36) blue char"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
